candidates.demo_desc: We've added 3 demo candidates so you can explore the platform. To get started with real candidates, use Import Excel to upload a spreadsheet or Invite Candidate to send individual invitations.

session.no_tasks_desc: Your school hasn't set up tasks for your grade yet. Please contact them.
session.paused_desc: We've emailed you a link to continue when you're ready.
session.transcribed: We transcribed what you said. Feel free to edit before submitting.
session.voice_error: We couldn't hear that clearly — try again or switch to typing.

onboarding.title: Get started with LIFT
onboarding.celebration_desc: LIFT is ready for your admissions cycle.
onboarding.step1_desc: Set up your grades, timeline, and candidate experience.
onboarding.step2_label: Invite an evaluator to your team
onboarding.step2_desc: Evaluators review candidate sessions and make recommendations.
onboarding.step3_desc: Send a secure link to a test candidate — even a colleague works.
onboarding.step4_desc: Have your test candidate complete the experience end to end.
onboarding.step5_desc: See what your evaluators will see — the TRI score, dimensions, and briefing.
help.school_admin.title: School Admin Guide
help.school_admin.subtitle: Everything you need to run your school's admissions process on LIFT.
help.dashboard.title: Dashboard Overview
help.dashboard.desc: Your dashboard gives you a real-time snapshot of your admissions pipeline. Here's what each section shows:
help.dashboard.stat_candidates: All candidates imported or invited to your school across all cycles. Includes active, completed, and flagged candidates.
help.dashboard.stat_sessions: Number of candidates who finished all assessment tasks. Each completed session automatically triggers the AI pipeline to generate insight profiles, TRI scores, and evaluator briefings.
help.dashboard.stat_flagged: Candidates whose AI-generated profiles were flagged for human review. This happens when confidence scores are low, unusual patterns are detected, or Learning Support Signals exceed thresholds. Always review these before making admissions decisions.
help.dashboard.stat_completion: The average percentage of tasks completed across all sessions. A number below 70% may indicate that candidates are dropping off mid-session — consider checking session settings or task difficulty.
help.dashboard.tip_review: The Review Queue shows candidates needing attention. Click any name to see their full profile, AI scores, and evaluator briefing.
help.candidates.title: Managing Candidates
help.candidates.import_1: Click "Import Excel" in the top right of the Candidates page.
help.candidates.import_2: Upload a spreadsheet (.xlsx or .csv) with columns: First Name, Last Name, Email, Grade Applying To, Date of Birth.
help.candidates.import_3: Review the import preview — LIFT will show you how many candidates will be created.
help.candidates.import_4: Click "Import" to create all candidates at once.
help.candidates.import_5: Each candidate gets an invite token automatically. Use "Invite Candidate" to send emails.
help.candidates.invite_1: Click "Invite Candidate" from the Candidates page.
help.candidates.invite_2: Fill in the candidate's name, email, grade applying to, and guardian info (if COPPA mode is on).
help.candidates.invite_3: Click "Send Invite" — the candidate receives an email with a secure session link.
help.candidates.invite_4: The link is valid for the number of days configured in your cycle settings.
help.candidates.status_invited: Invite sent, waiting for candidate to open the link
help.candidates.status_active: Session in progress or ready to begin
help.candidates.status_completed: All tasks finished, AI pipeline has run
help.candidates.status_flagged: Requires human review before admissions decision
help.candidates.demo_tip: When you first register, LIFT creates 3 demo candidates so you can explore the platform. These are marked with "(Demo)" and can be deleted anytime.
help.cycles.title: Admissions Cycles
help.cycles.desc: An admissions cycle represents one application period (e.g. "Fall 2026"). Each cycle has its own candidate pool, dates, and grades.
help.cycles.create_1: Click "New Cycle" from the Cycles page.
help.cycles.create_2: Enter a name (e.g. "Fall 2026 Admissions") and academic year.
help.cycles.create_3: Set opening and closing dates — invites can only be sent during this window.
help.cycles.create_4: Configure grades (6-7, 8, 9-11) — each grade gets its own set of age-appropriate assessment tasks.
help.cycles.create_5: Click "Create Cycle" — it becomes active immediately.
help.cycles.tip: You can have multiple cycles but only one active at a time. Completed cycles are archived and their data remains accessible for reporting.
help.team.title: Team Management
help.team.desc: Add evaluators, interviewers, and other staff to your school. Each role sees a different view of the platform.
help.team.role_admin: Full control: candidates, cycles, team, settings, billing. You are this role.
help.team.role_evaluator: Reviews candidate profiles, TRI scores, AI briefings. Writes evaluator reviews and recommendations.
help.team.role_interviewer: Conducts interviews using AI-generated briefings. Submits rubric scores and interview notes.
help.team.invite_2: Enter their name, email, and select a role.
help.team.invite_3: They receive an email with a link to set up their account.
help.team.invite_4: Once they log in, they see only the pages relevant to their role.
help.team.warning_seats: Evaluator and interviewer seats are limited by your subscription tier. Check your plan limits in Settings → Subscription.
help.evaluator_workspace.title: Understanding the Evaluator View
help.evaluator_workspace.desc: When a candidate completes their session, LIFT generates an insight profile that evaluators use for review.
help.evaluator_workspace.tri_desc: A composite score (0-100) across 6 dimensions: reading, writing, reasoning, reflection, persistence, and support-seeking. Labels: Emerging (<40), Developing (40-59), Ready (60-79), Thriving (80+).
help.evaluator_workspace.dimensions_desc: Individual scores (0-100) for each dimension, generated by AI analyzing the candidate's actual responses. Each includes a confidence level and rationale.
help.evaluator_workspace.briefing_desc: An AI-generated pre-interview guide with 3-5 observations, 6-8 tailored interview questions, and areas to explore further.
help.evaluator_workspace.lss_desc: Behavioral patterns that may indicate a need for learning support evaluation. Three levels: None (0-1 flags), Watch (2-3), Recommend Screening (4+). This is NOT a diagnosis.
help.settings.title: School Settings
help.subscription.title: Subscription & Billing
help.stats.title: Stats & Scores Explained
help.evaluator.title: Evaluator Guide
help.evaluator.subtitle: How to review candidates, interpret AI-generated insights, and write effective evaluations.
help.interviewer.title: Interviewer Guide
help.interviewer.subtitle: How to prepare for interviews, use AI briefings, score rubrics, and submit notes.
help.admin.title: Platform Admin Guide
help.admin.subtitle: Managing tenants, licenses, revenue, and platform operations.
candidates.name: Name
session.write_here: Write your response here. Take your time...
session.show_work: Show your work — explain how you got your answer
session.explain_thinking: Explain your thinking step by step...
session.no_wrong_answers: There are no right or wrong answers. Share your honest thoughts.
settings.language_desc: Language for invites and session UI
settings.coppa_desc: Require guardian consent for all new invites
settings.session_pause_desc: Allow candidates to pause and resume sessions
settings.data_retention_desc: How long candidate data is kept
settings.human_review_desc: Flag every insight profile for manual review
settings.voice_response_desc: Allow candidates to speak their responses instead of typing. Recommended for Grade 6-7.
settings.passage_reader_desc: Read passages aloud to candidates. Helps with reading difficulties without affecting scoring.
settings.audio_privacy: Voice recordings are transcribed and immediately deleted. No audio is stored after transcription.
analytics.candidates_info: Total candidates imported or invited this cycle
analytics.sessions_info: Candidates who finished all assessment tasks
analytics.rate_info: Percentage of invited candidates who completed their session
analytics.tri_info: Average Transition Readiness Index across all completed sessions
analytics.usage_info: Sessions consumed vs your plan limit
analytics.time_info: Average time candidates spend completing all tasks
mission_banner.title: Add your school mission statement
mission_banner.body: Decision-language rationale ties to your mission when this field is set. Without it, language falls back to generic school-name phrasing.
flags.desc.consent_not_captured: Candidate is admitted, waitlisted, or offered, but required consent types were not observed in the consent log.
flags.desc.invite_expired_unopened: The most recent invite expired without being opened. No record of the recipient viewing it.
flags.desc.assessment_abandoned: The assessment session was explicitly abandoned or has been inactive for more than 7 days without completion.
flags.desc.low_completion: Candidate completed the assessment but finished fewer than 75% of the tasks.
flags.desc.late_cycle_admit: The admit or waitlist decision was recorded within 7 days of the application cycle's close date.
flags.desc.post_admit_silence: Candidate was admitted and we have observed no activity — consent, invite, status change, assignment, or application update — within the tenant's configured silence window.
flags.desc.interviewer_unresponsive: An interview assignment was recorded more than 14 days ago and no rubric has been submitted.
flags.no_active: No active flags on this candidate.
flags.drawer.subtitle: Observed conditions on this candidate. Not predictions of outcome.
flags.resolve.reason_placeholder: e.g. Family confirmed enrollment by phone
flags.resolve.snooze_label: Snooze window ({days} days — flag re-raises if condition persists)
interviewer_prep.empty: No candidates assigned for interview yet. Your school admin will assign candidates as they complete their assessments and become ready for committee review.
flags.page.title: Enrollment Readiness Flags
flags.page.subtitle: Observed conditions that may need admissions-team attention. Each flag is raised from real data on the candidate — never a prediction of outcome.
flags.page.spec_link: See the per-flag spec for every flag's raise condition.
flags.page.feature_disabled_body: Enrollment Readiness Flags are not enabled on this tier.
flags.empty.no_match: No active flags match the current filters.
dl.title: Decision Language
dl.empty_precompletion: This candidate hasn't completed their assessment yet. Decision language is generated automatically once the assessment pipeline finishes. Current status:
dl.empty_postcompletion: Decision language will be generated automatically after the assessment pipeline completes. If the pipeline has already finished for this candidate, you can trigger a manual generation below.
dl.banner_pre_committee_title: Not for external use until committee review is complete.
dl.banner_pre_committee_body: This language is pre-drafted for reference only — do not send to families until the admissions committee has deliberated this case.
dl.banner_fallback_title: Safe template used. Review before sending.
dl.banner_fallback_body: AI generation did not pass the content guardrails on one or more versions; a deterministic safe template was substituted. Review the text carefully before any external use.
dl.readonly_notice: Read-only view. Decision language is an admissions-authority output. Only school administrators can copy, edit, download, or regenerate.
dl.using_edited: Using edited version · most recent edit shown
committee.defer_template: Decision deferred pending additional information. The admissions team will revisit this candidate at a subsequent committee session once the outstanding items have been resolved.
committee.stale_language_body: Your mission statement has been updated since this was generated. The committee can still deliberate; regenerate to refresh before sending.
committee.rationale_placeholder: Why this decision? (captured in audit, not sent to family)
committee.observer_mode: No vote recorded yet. Observer mode — only the host can record.
end_session.review_instructions: Review each decision. Use "Hold" to keep a candidate staged for the next session. On commit, final_recommendations writes per candidate. For admits, CORE handoff, support plan generation, and SIS sync fire automatically.
end_session.admit_warning: {count} admit decision{plural} will fire CORE handoff, support plan generation, and SIS sync for each candidate.
invite.invalid_title: This link is no longer valid
invite.invalid_body: This invitation may have expired or already been used. Please contact your school's admissions office for a new link.
invite.invited_body_prefix: has invited you to complete the LIFT experience — a short set of reading, writing, and reasoning activities.
invite.not_a_test: This is not a test. There are no right or wrong answers. We're interested in how you think and approach different tasks.
invite.duration_hint: It typically takes 30-50 minutes. You can pause and come back if needed.
briefing.page_subtitle: Candidates ready for committee review, with generated decision language status.
briefing.empty: No candidates are ready for committee review yet.
briefing.select_all_aria: Select all candidates ready for committee
session_page.end_session_disabled_toolti: Record at least one vote before ending
auth.login_tagline: Admissions intelligence. Built for the humans who decide.
auth.forgot_body: Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
auth.forgot_sent_body: If an account exists for that email, we've sent a reset link. It expires in 1 hour.
auth.reset_body: Enter your new password below. It must be at least 8 characters.
auth.reset_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters
consent.waiting_guardian_sent_prefix: We've sent a consent request to
consent.waiting_guardian_approved: Once your guardian approves, you'll be able to start your session. Check back soon!
consent.guardian_required_body: Because you're under 13, a parent or guardian needs to give permission before you can begin.
consent.what_is_lift_body: LIFT is a set of short activities that explore how you approach reading, writing, and reasoning tasks. {school} uses these insights as part of their admissions process.
consent.not_a_test_body: There are no right or wrong answers. We're interested in how you think — not what you know. Take your time, do your best, and be yourself.
consent.disclaimer: LIFT is a non-diagnostic platform. It does not diagnose, screen for, or identify any clinical, medical, or learning condition. The insights generated are used solely to help schools understand how candidates approach learning tasks. All final decisions are made by qualified human reviewers.
consent.agree_checkbox_label: I understand that LIFT is not a test or diagnosis, and I agree to participate.
guardian.thank_you_body: You've given consent for {name} to participate in the LIFT experience at {school}. They can now begin their session.
guardian.title: Guardian Consent
guardian.intro: Hello {guardian}, {school} has invited {name} to complete the LIFT experience as part of their admissions process.
guardian.what_is_lift_body: LIFT is a set of short activities that explore how students approach reading, writing, and reasoning tasks. It is not a test — there are no right or wrong answers.
guardian.disclaimer_body: LIFT does not diagnose, screen for, or identify any clinical, medical, or learning condition. All insights are reviewed by qualified human evaluators before any admissions decision is made.
guardian.agree_checkbox: I give consent for {name} to participate in the LIFT experience. I understand this is not a diagnosis or clinical assessment.
session_ui.placeholder_extended: Write your response here. Take your time…
session_ui.placeholder_work: Explain your thinking step by step…
session_ui.reflection_encouragement: There are no right or wrong answers. Share your honest thoughts.
session_ui.show_your_work_label: Show your work — explain how you got your answer
session_ui.explain_figure_out_label: Explain how you figured it out
eval_detail.tri_what_body: The Transition Readiness Index (TRI) is a composite score (0-100) measuring readiness across 6 dimensions: Reading (20%), Writing (20%), Reasoning (20%), Reflection (15%), Persistence (15%), and Support Seeking (10%). Scores are adjusted for AI confidence and learning support signals.
eval_detail.signals_banner_body: Signals are behavioral data captured during the candidate's session. They show HOW the candidate approached the tasks — not just what they wrote. Use these alongside responses to understand engagement, pace, and help-seeking patterns.
eval_detail.signals_avg_time_desc: Average time spent on each task. Very short times may indicate rushing. Very long times may indicate difficulty.
eval_detail.signals_longest_desc: The most time spent on a single task. Identifies which task type challenged this candidate most.
eval_detail.signals_duration_desc: Total time from first task to last submission. Provides context for the overall pace of the session.
eval_detail.signals_time_per_task_desc: How long the candidate spent on each task. Longer bars indicate more time spent.
eval_detail.review_prompt_with_ai: Review the AI recommendation above, then provide your own assessment.
eval_detail.review_prompt_no_ai: Provide your assessment of this candidate.
eval_detail.review_differs_from_ai: Your recommendation differs from the AI
